title: Hylotelephium maritimum
taxonomy: Hylotelephium maritimum is classified within the plant family Crassulaceae. This succulent plant is native to China. It is also known as Sedum maritimum. The classification of Hylotelephium maritimum is further detailed at the genus and species levels. At the higher taxonomic level, it is classified as part of the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Hylotelephium maritimum, also known as sea stonecrop or sea sedum, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help soothe skin irritations and wounds. Some studies have also suggested that Hylotelephium maritimum may have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal uses and potential side effects.
